topic: Harness Tool-Surface Audit — the 'what ships with what' matrix

sources.title: Claude Code — Tools reference (2026)
keyInsights: Claude Code ships 30+ built-in tools in 2026: Agent, AskUserQuestion, Bash, CronCreate/List/Delete, Edit, EnterPlanMode/ExitPlanMode, EnterWorktree/ExitWorktree, Glob, Grep, ListMcpResourcesTool, LSP, Monitor, NotebookEdit, PowerShell, Read, ReadMcpResourceTool, SendMessage, Skill, TaskCreate/Get/List/Update/Stop, TeamCreate/Delete, TodoWrite (deprecated), ToolSearch, WebFetch, WebSearch, Write
Monitor tool (v2.1.98+) watches a background command and feeds each output line to the model — unique to Claude Code
EnterWorktree / ExitWorktree are first-class worktree tools — nobody else has this
LSP tool requires a plugin per language; gives jump-to-def, find-refs, type info, symbol listing, call-hierarchy
PowerShell tool is a preview non-Bash shell; opt-in on non-Windows, rolling out on Windows
TodoWrite is marked deprecated in favor of TaskCreate/Get/List/Update — the task-list surface has expanded

sources.title: Claude Agent SDK — Overview
keyInsights: Agent SDK ships the same tool surface as Claude Code but exposes a narrower default — allowed_tools in practice picks a subset (Read, Edit, Bash, Glob, Grep, WebSearch, WebFetch, AskUserQuestion, Monitor are the highlighted ones)
Monitor is explicitly named as part of the SDK's built-in tools
AskUserQuestion is the structured multi-choice clarification tool; shipped in both TS and Python
Subagents invoked via Agent tool; Agent must be in allowedTools
The TS SDK bundles the Claude Code binary as an optional dependency

sources.title: OpenAI Codex CLI — features
keyInsights: Codex CLI ships 6 model-facing tools: shell/container.exec, apply_patch, update_plan, view_image, write_stdout, web_search
web_search defaults to cached; live requires --search flag or config
All tools respect approval modes (Auto/Read-only/Full Access) and sandbox settings
No dedicated Read, Write, Edit, Glob, or Grep — everything routes through shell + apply_patch
MCP integration is separate from the built-in tool set
The deliberate minimalism contrasts directly with Claude Code's 30+ tools

sources.title: OpenAI Agents SDK — Tools
keyInsights: Hosted tools: WebSearchTool, FileSearchTool (vector stores), CodeInterpreterTool, HostedMCPTool, ImageGenerationTool, ToolSearchTool
Local tools: ComputerTool (GUI/browser), ShellTool, LocalShellTool (legacy), ApplyPatchTool
Function tools: @function_tool decorator, FunctionTool class, tool_namespace() for grouping
Agent.as_tool() exposes any Agent as a callable tool — the SDK's handoff primitive
codex_tool (experimental) runs workspace-scoped Codex tasks from inside an agent
FileSearchTool is NOT filesystem grep — it's vector-store retrieval; a common conflation
defer_loading flag on function_tool / HostedMCPTool / ToolSearchTool enables ToolSearch-based lazy loading

sources.title: OpenCode — Tools
keyInsights: OpenCode ships 13 built-in tools: bash, edit, write, read, grep, glob, lsp (experimental), apply_patch, skill, todowrite, webfetch, websearch, question
lsp tool is experimental; OpenCode is the only harness besides Claude Code with first-class LSP
websearch uses Exa AI for the search backend
question is OpenCode's name for the structured ask-user primitive; simpler schema than Claude Code's AskUserQuestion
skill tool loads a skill file and returns its content — similar to Claude Code's Skill
Tool names are lowercase, breaking from Claude Code's PascalCase convention

sources.title: Cline — Tools guide
keyInsights: Cline ships: write_to_file, read_file, replace_in_file, search_files, list_files, list_code_definition_names, execute_command, browser_action, use_mcp_tool, access_mcp_resource, ask_followup_question, attempt_completion, new_task
All tools use XML tag parameter conventions (<path>...</path>), not JSON schema
replace_in_file is the targeted-edit tool; write_to_file is the full-file-overwrite fallback
browser_action wraps Puppeteer; Cline is one of the few to ship browser as a first-class tool
attempt_completion is the finish-task primitive (vs Claude Code's stop_reason:end_turn)
new_task spawns a fresh task with a clean context window

sources.title: Roo Code — how tools work
keyInsights: Roo Code (Cline fork) has 5 edit tools: apply_diff, edit_file, search_replace, edit, apply_patch — each with slightly different semantics
Adds codebase_search (semantic), apply_patch (multi-file unified diffs), generate_image, switch_mode, update_todo_list
read_command_output retrieves truncated output from previous commands
new_task creates a subtask with a specific starting mode
skill tool loads predefined skill instructions
The 5-edit-tool fragmentation is a cautionary example: more tools != better coverage

sources.title: Cursor — Agent tools
keyInsights: Cursor agent has 8 tools: Semantic Search, Search Files and Folders, Read Files, Edit Files, Run Shell Commands, Web, Browser, Image Generation
Semantic Search is first-class; Cursor is the only IDE-side harness with semantic search as a primary tool (vs Claude Code which relies on Grep)
Read Files supports vision — images are first-class
Browser tool controls a real browser for screenshots, navigation, visual verification
Image Generation saves to project assets/ folder
Also has Fetch Rules (retrieve project rules) and Ask Questions — listed as agent capabilities but not explicitly in the 8

sources.title: Gemini CLI — tools directory
keyInsights: Gemini CLI ships ~20 tools: read-file, read-many-files, list-directory, ls, glob, grep, ripGrep, search_file_content, edit, write-file, shell, shellBackgroundTools, web-fetch, web-search, save-memory, ask-user, activate-skill, complete-task, enter-plan-mode, exit-plan-mode, write-todos, topicTool, trackerTools
THREE separate grep-style tools (grep, ripGrep, search_file_content) — redundant and a design smell
save_memory is unique: persistent memory as a first-class tool
enter-plan-mode / exit-plan-mode mirror Claude Code's pair
activate-skill / list-mcp-resources / read-mcp-resource / mcp-tool / mcp-client are a rich MCP-adjacent surface

sources.title: MCP filesystem reference server
keyInsights: MCP filesystem ships 13 tools: read_text_file (with head/tail), read_media_file, read_multiple_files, write_file, edit_file (with dryRun), create_directory, list_directory, list_directory_with_sizes, directory_tree, move_file, search_files, get_file_info, list_allowed_directories
edit_file is the only MCP-FS edit tool; accepts an array of {oldText, newText} edits and supports dryRun
search_files is misleadingly named — it's a FIND-BY-PATTERN tool (glob-style name matching), NOT a content grep
list_directory_with_sizes supports sortBy: name|size
read_text_file supports head OR tail but not both simultaneously
No content-grep equivalent; users are expected to run grep via a separate MCP server or shell

sources.title: MCP git reference server
keyInsights: MCP git server ships 12 tools: git_status, git_diff_unstaged, git_diff_staged, git_diff, git_commit, git_add, git_reset, git_log, git_create_branch, git_checkout, git_show, git_branch
Alternative to shell-git; gives the model structured git operations without shell injection risk
No harness ships git operations as first-class tools; all delegate to either shell+git OR this MCP server

sources.title: MCP GitHub server (archived)
keyInsights: Exposed 26 tools for GitHub API: create_or_update_file, push_files, search_repositories, create_repository, get_file_contents, fork_repository, create_branch, list_commits, create_issue, list_issues, update_issue, get_issue, add_issue_comment, create_pull_request, list_pull_requests, get_pull_request, get_pull_request_files, get_pull_request_status, merge_pull_request, update_pull_request_branch, get_pull_request_comments, get_pull_request_reviews, create_pull_request_review, search_code, search_issues, search_users
Shows the richness MCP can add beyond a harness's native surface
Archived but widely forked — still the reference shape

sources.title: MCP reference servers (overview)
keyInsights: Active reference servers: Everything (test), Fetch (web), Filesystem, Git, Memory (knowledge graph), Sequential Thinking, Time
Archived: GitHub, GitLab, PostgreSQL, SQLite, Puppeteer, Brave Search — still widely used but no longer actively maintained in this repo
Memory server uses a knowledge-graph entities/relations/observations model, different from Gemini CLI's save_memory (key-value)
Sequential Thinking is essentially a 'show your work' tool — the model calls it to reason

sources.title: LangChain FileManagementToolkit
keyInsights: LangChain's FileManagementToolkit ships 7 tools: CopyFileTool, DeleteFileTool, FileSearchTool, MoveFileTool, ReadFileTool, WriteFileTool, ListDirectoryTool
All tools scoped to a root_dir parameter (critical: non-sandboxed otherwise)
selected_tools list lets you enable only a subset
No edit-in-place tool; only full-file write
FileSearchTool is a name-search (like MCP-FS's search_files), not a content grep
Documentation explicitly warns: 'not recommended for use outside a sandboxed environment'

sources.title: CrewAI tools overview
keyInsights: CrewAI has 40+ tools organized by category: File/Document, Search/Research, AI/ML, Cloud/Storage, Web, Automation, Database, Integrations
Core file tools: FileReadTool, FileWriterTool, DirectoryReadTool
Search: SerperDevTool (Google), CodeDocsSearchTool, WebsiteSearchTool
AI/ML: RAG Tool, Code Interpreter Tool, DALL-E Integration
Integration-heavy (S3 Reader, Scrape Website, etc.) — the catalog is broad but shallow on the file/code-editing side

sources.title: Continue — tool definitions
keyInsights: Continue ships 21 tool definitions: codebaseTool, createNewFile, createRuleBlock, editFile, fetchUrlContent, globSearch, grepSearch, ls, multiEdit, readCurrentlyOpenFile, readFile, readFileRange, readSkill, requestRule, runTerminalCommand, searchWeb, singleFindAndReplace, viewDiff, viewRepoMap, viewSubdirectory
Three separate read tools: readFile, readFileRange, readCurrentlyOpenFile — schema-differentiated by use case
Edit surface has 3 tools: editFile (natural-language), multiEdit, singleFindAndReplace — similar Roo-style fragmentation
Unique: viewDiff, viewRepoMap, viewSubdirectory as first-class tools (read-only IDE-panel adapters)
createRuleBlock / requestRule are IDE-integration tools for Continue's rules system
codebaseTool is semantic retrieval over indexed codebase

sources.title: Pydantic AI common tools
keyInsights: Pydantic-AI ships: duckduckgo_search_tool, web_fetch_tool (with SSRF protection built-in), tavily_search_tool (paid), Exa search toolkit (search/find_similar/content/answer)
web_fetch_tool converts HTML to markdown automatically
SSRF protection is a rarity — most harness WebFetch implementations don't have it
Tools are added to agents via tools or toolsets parameters
No filesystem tools — Pydantic-AI is a framework, not a harness

sources.title: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore — introduction
keyInsights: Bedrock AgentCore is a platform, not a tool library — modular services: Runtime, Memory, Code Interpreter, Browser, Gateway, Identity, Observability
Gateway turns existing APIs and Lambda functions into agent tools via MCP — the AWS equivalent of 'MCP everything'
Code Interpreter provides a sandboxed Python execution environment
Browser provides managed web browser instances at scale
Memory maintains short-term and long-term context across sessions
Services work modularly or independently
Distinct from Bedrock Agents (the older Action Groups + Knowledge Bases product)

sources.title: Aider — commands
keyInsights: Aider exposes ~50 slash commands but ZERO model-facing tools in the function-calling sense
Commands include: /add, /drop, /read-only, /run, /test, /lint, /git, /diff, /commit, /code, /ask, /architect, /clear, /reset, /tokens, /undo, /help, /web, /edit, /ls
/architect switches to dual-model planner+editor mode
/web scrapes a URL and adds it to context as markdown
The entire model interface is text-based: search/replace blocks, unified diffs, whole-file, diff-fenced — parsed by the harness after the turn
Deliberate architectural stance: function calling is the wrong interface for code editing

sources.title: SWE-agent docs (index)
keyInsights: SWE-agent ACI tools (default config): open, goto, edit, scroll_up, scroll_down, search_dir, search_file, find_file, create, submit
Stateful tool design: open + goto + scroll maintain a 'current file + current line' state machine
edit uses N:M<<<EOF...EOF syntax (line range + heredoc body) with optional lint gate on save
submit is the finish-task primitive (vs stop_reason or attempt_completion)
filemap tool prints a Python file skipping function bodies — compression for large files
Benchmark-tuned narrow surface — proves that 10 tools is enough for SWE-bench tasks

sources.title: Armin Ronacher — Agentic Coding Tools
keyInsights: Tools should be designed assuming an 'LLM chaos monkey' — every tool called with unexpected args
Crashes are acceptable, hangs are problematic — every tool needs a timeout
Verification tools (tests, linters, LSP, compilation) are the highest-leverage thing an agent can have
Go / plain SQL / descriptive function names beat magical Python for agent work
Harness design forces you to build provider-neutral abstractions; cache control and tool prompting diverge across providers
Reinforcement: inject contextual reminders into tool outputs to re-anchor the model on its goal during long loops

sources.title: AutoGPT classic abilities (historical reference)
keyInsights: Classic AutoGPT shipped: read_file, write_file, append_to_file, list_files, delete_file, web_search, browse_website, execute_python_code, execute_shell, image_gen, improve_code, write_tests
The 'classic' project is UNSUPPORTED and contains known vulnerabilities — educational only
The current AutoGPT Platform is a block-based integration catalog (Firecrawl, Jina, Exa, GitHub, Google Docs, Notion, Discord, Airtable, HubSpot, Twitter) rather than a local agent harness
As a reference for 'what tools did the early autonomous agents ship,' this is the canonical list
Not comparable to 2026 harnesses — included for historical completeness only

gaps: OpenHands CodeActAgent exact tool schemas not verified from source — relied on paper/doc summaries and cross-harness references. The action space (IPythonRunCellAction, CmdRunAction, FileReadAction, FileEditAction, FileWriteAction, BrowseURLAction, BrowseInteractiveAction, MessageAction, AgentFinishAction, AgentDelegateAction, AgentThinkAction) is inferred from prior-art knowledge and not re-verified in this pass.
Cline's load_mcp_documentation and focus_chain not confirmed in the tools-guide fetch; may be newer features or removed
SWE-agent's exact default-config tool list not re-confirmed in this pass; relied on widely-known ACI design from the paper
Microsoft AutoGen / Magentic-One tool schemas only at a high level (FileSurfer, WebSurfer, Coder, ComputerTerminal); not schema-level
Bedrock Agents (the older Action Groups product) listed only conceptually; not matrixed separately from AgentCore
LangChain has moved a lot of tools to langchain-community and langchain-experimental; the full inventory there is not re-verified
